api.purpose: Public API surface for OpenMeter: TypeSpec source (api/spec/) is the single source of truth that compiles to OpenAPI YAMLs, Go server stubs (api/api.gen.go, api/v3/api.gen.go), and Go/JS/Python SDKs (api/client/). All downstream artefacts are generated — never edit them directly. Primary constraint: any new endpoint must start in TypeSpec, flow through make gen-api, then make generate before a handler is written.

patterns.name: Two-step regeneration mandatory
patterns.description: TypeSpec changes require make gen-api (regenerates OpenAPI YAMLs + SDKs) then make generate (regenerates Go server stubs, Wire, Ent). Both steps must run and be committed together.

patterns.name: Generated-file guard
patterns.description: Files with 'Code generated by X, DO NOT EDIT.' headers (api/api.gen.go, api/v3/api.gen.go, api/client/go/client.gen.go) must never be hand-edited. Edit api/spec/ then regenerate.
patterns.example: // Code generated by github.com/oapi-codegen/oapi-codegen/v2 ... DO NOT EDIT.

patterns.name: v1 vs v3 package split
patterns.description: v1 API belongs in api/spec/packages/legacy/ producing api/api.gen.go; v3 AIP API belongs in api/spec/packages/aip/ producing api/v3/api.gen.go. Never mix content between versions.
patterns.example: api/spec/packages/aip/src/openmeter.tsp for v3; api/spec/packages/legacy/src/main.tsp for v1

patterns.name: Route and tag bindings at root namespace only
patterns.description: @route and @tag decorators must only appear in root openmeter.tsp (v3) or main.tsp (v1), never inside domain sub-folder operation files.

patterns.name: Error responses via apierrors package
patterns.description: All v3 error responses must use the apierrors package for RFC 7807 compliance. Direct http.Error or w.WriteHeader calls are forbidden in handlers.

patterns.name: api/types for manual gap-fill types only
patterns.description: Only types that oapi-codegen cannot generate (oneOf/anyOf schemas) live in api/types via x-go-type references. No business logic, helpers, or methods go here.
patterns.example: // api/types/doc.go — pure type definitions referenced from openapi spec via x-go-type

patterns.name: nullable.Nullable[T] for optional JSON fields in v3
patterns.description: v3 codegen uses nullable-type:true — use nullable.Nullable[string] not *string for optional JSON fields to preserve the null vs absent distinction.

key_file_guides.role: Generated v1 server stubs, enums, request/response types from openapi.yaml via oapi-codegen
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerate via make gen-api; hand edits are silently overwritten

key_file_guides.role: Generated v3 server stubs from api/v3/openapi.yaml via oapi-codegen with chi-server and nullable types
key_file_guides.watch_for: DO NOT EDIT — regenerate via make gen-api; uses always-prefix-enum-values so enum consts are prefixed

key_file_guides.role: oapi-codegen config for v3: nullable-type=true, always-prefix-enum-values, custom chi-middleware.tmpl for deepObject filters
key_file_guides.watch_for: custom chi-middleware.tmpl patch must stay aligned with codegen.yaml settings when upgrading oapi-codegen

key_file_guides.role: v1 TypeSpec entry point; all new legacy sub-domain .tsp files must be registered here AND in cloud/main.tsp
key_file_guides.watch_for: Missing registration means endpoint is not generated — check both main.tsp and cloud/main.tsp

key_file_guides.role: v3 AIP TypeSpec entry point; @route and @tag bindings are declared here only, not in domain sub-folder files
key_file_guides.watch_for: @route and @tag must NOT appear inside domain sub-folder TypeSpec operation files

key_file_guides.role: Generated Go SDK from api/openapi.cloud.yaml (not openapi.yaml) — cloud variant exposes additional cloud-only endpoints
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must not import internal monorepo packages — stays externally importable; regenerated from cloud spec only

key_file_guides.role: Generated v1 OpenAPI spec — downstream artifact of make gen-api from TypeSpec
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never hand-edit; always run make gen-api after TypeSpec changes

key_file_guides.role: Post-processing script that flattens allOf schemas in the v3 OpenAPI output
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must run as part of make gen-api pipeline; bypassing it leaves unflattened allOf in api/v3/openapi.yaml

anti_patterns: Hand-editing api/openapi.yaml, api/openapi.cloud.yaml, api/v3/openapi.yaml, or any *.gen.go file — always regenerate via make gen-api
Adding @route or @tag inside domain sub-folder TypeSpec files — routing belongs in root namespace files (openmeter.tsp / main.tsp) only
Adding business logic inside api/v3/ outside handlers/ sub-packages — api/v3 is infrastructure only; all domain calls belong in api/v3/handlers/*
Adding new legacy sub-domain TypeSpec file without registering it in both main.tsp and cloud/main.tsp — endpoint silently absent
Using *string instead of nullable.Nullable[string] for optional JSON fields in v3 — produces incorrect null vs absent distinction

decisions.decision: TypeSpec as single source of truth compiling to OpenAPI + multi-language SDKs
decisions.rationale: One spec feeds Go, JS, and Python SDKs plus two server versions — eliminates cross-language drift and forces breaking changes to be caught at TypeSpec compile time

decisions.decision: Go SDK generated from api/openapi.cloud.yaml, not openapi.yaml
decisions.rationale: Cloud variant exposes additional cloud-only endpoints not present in the self-hosted spec; keeps self-hosted and cloud SDKs correctly scoped

decisions.decision: oapi-codegen with nullable-type:true and always-prefix-enum-values for v3
decisions.rationale: Correct null vs absent distinction for optional JSON fields; prefixed enums prevent name collisions across the large v3 type namespace

code_examples.scenario: Add a v3 API endpoint: author TypeSpec then implement handler

openmeter.purpose: Core business logic monorepo root: organises all domain packages (billing, customer, entitlement, subscription, meter, ingest, sink, notification, ledger, watermill, etc.) each following a strict service/adapter/httpdriver layering. No direct source files live here — all code is in sub-packages. Primary constraint: domain packages must not import app/common or cmd/* to remain independently testable and wirable.

patterns.name: Layered service/adapter/httpdriver split per domain
patterns.description: Every domain exposes a Service interface at the package root (service.go), a concrete implementation in service/, an Adapter interface at root, an Ent/PostgreSQL implementation in adapter/, and HTTP handlers in httpdriver/ or httphandler/. Business logic in service/, persistence in adapter/, HTTP in httpdriver/.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo for all Ent adapter writes
patterns.description: Every Ent adapter method body wraps DB access with entutils.TransactingRepo / TransactingRepoWithNoValue so the ctx-bound Ent transaction is honored. Raw *entdb.Client helpers must also wrap.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) (*Entity, error) { return toDomain(tx.db.Entity.Create().Save(ctx)) })

patterns.name: Input.Validate() at every service boundary
patterns.description: All input types crossing a service boundary implement Validate() and are called before any business logic. Validation errors are returned as models.Generic* types or ValidationIssue sentinels.
patterns.example: if err := input.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: ServiceHooks and RequestValidatorRegistry for cross-domain callbacks
patterns.description: Cross-domain lifecycle reactions (billing hooking customer lifecycle, ledger hooking customer creation) must register via ServiceHooks or RequestValidatorRegistry in app/common, never via direct import of the other domain's service.

patterns.name: credits.enabled guarded at four independent wiring layers
patterns.description: When credits.enabled=false, each of these must independently skip: (1) ledger services in app/common/ledger.go, (2) customer ledger hooks in app/common/customer.go, (3) ChargesRegistry in app/common/billing.go, (4) v3 credit handlers in api/v3/server. No single guard suffices.

patterns.name: clock.Now() everywhere, never time.Now()
patterns.description: All production code must call clock.Now() (import github.com/openmeterio/openmeter/pkg/clock) so tests can use FreezeTime/UnFreeze for deterministic time-dependent billing calculations.

patterns.name: Domain test helpers isolated from app/common
patterns.description: Test fixtures under openmeter/<domain>/testutils/ must build deps from package constructors, never importing app/common — avoids import cycles. Use openmeter/testutils for shared infrastructure helpers.
patterns.example: // openmeter/billing/testutils — builds from billing.New, adapter.New directly, never app/common

key_file_guides.role: Composite billing.Service interface contract — no business logic here; implementation in billing/service/
key_file_guides.watch_for: Add new methods to the interface here; implementation goes in billing/service/service.go

key_file_guides.role: Single codegen driver for all Ent-generated code in openmeter/ent/db/
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never edit openmeter/ent/db/ — it is fully generated by make generate; edit schemas in openmeter/ent/schema/

key_file_guides.role: Single Publisher facade routing events to ingest/system/balance-worker Kafka topics by event-name prefix (EventVersionSubsystem constant)
key_file_guides.watch_for: Always publish via eventbus.Publisher, never to raw topic strings; unknown prefixes silently route to SystemEventsTopic

key_file_guides.role: RequestValidatorRegistry for pre-mutation cross-domain validation guards (billing, entitlement → customer)
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validators registered here in app/common; post-mutation side-effects belong in ServiceHooks, not here

key_file_guides.role: Manager fans out CreateNamespace/DeleteNamespace to all registered Handlers
key_file_guides.watch_for: Register namespace Handlers BEFORE CreateDefaultNamespace is called at startup in cmd/server/main.go

key_file_guides.role: Kafka→ClickHouse sink worker with exactly-once flush ordering
key_file_guides.watch_for: Flush order must be: ClickHouse BatchInsert → Kafka offset commit → Redis dedupe; FlushEventHandler must run in a goroutine, never synchronously

key_file_guides.role: Zero-value noop implementations of all ledger interfaces used when credits.enabled=false
key_file_guides.watch_for: Wire noop here when credits disabled; do NOT wire nil to a ledger interface — panics at call sites

key_file_guides.role: Shared Postgres test database provisioning via pgtestdb — skips when POSTGRES_HOST unset
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must never import app/common; always use t.Context() not context.Background() in helpers

anti_patterns: Importing app/common from any openmeter/<domain>/ or openmeter/testutils/ package — creates import cycles that Wire cannot resolve
Using time.Now() instead of clock.Now() in production code — breaks test time freezing for billing calculations
Adding business logic or DB queries directly to service.go or adapter.go interface files — logic belongs in service/ and adapter/ sub-packages
Calling sub-type connectors or adapters directly from HTTP handlers — always go through the top-level Service interface
Registering ledger namespace handlers or customer ledger hooks when credits.enabled=false — must use noop implementations to avoid real DB writes

decisions.decision: Domain packages have no dependency on cmd/* or app/common
decisions.rationale: Keeps domain packages independently testable and prevents Wire graph complexity from leaking into business logic; hooks are registered via app/common provider side-effects to avoid circular imports

decisions.decision: ServiceHook / RequestValidatorRegistry pattern for cross-domain callbacks
decisions.rationale: Avoids circular imports between billing, customer, ledger, and entitlement while still allowing lifecycle reactions; registrations happen as side-effects in app/common Wire providers

decisions.decision: credits.enabled guarded at four independent wiring layers rather than one central check
decisions.rationale: Credits touches HTTP handlers, customer hooks, namespace provisioning, and charge creation across unrelated call graphs — no single choke point controls all write paths

code_examples.scenario: New domain adapter method with full TransactingRepo transaction awareness
